auditor: Tombe Herrington Chartered Professional Accountants LLP
economic_notes: Investments in non-controlled entities include Cariboo Indian Enterprises Ltd., Tsilhqot'in Economic Development Corp., Tsilhqot'in Forest Products Inc., Tsilhqot'in Economic Advancement GP Ltd. and Dandzen Development Limited Partnership. Band has won Supreme Court of Canada ruling on aboriginal rights and title. Legal services accumulated of $36,389,000 for asserting aboriginal title contingent on outside funding.
notes: Audit is qualified due to Social Housing tangible capital assets being amortized at a rate equivalent to annual principal reduction of related long-term debt as required by CMHC, which is not in accordance with Canadian public sector accounting standards. Effect on net book value, amortization expense, annual surplus and equity in tangible capital assets has not been determined. Consolidated entity includes 1224366 B.C. Ltd. Five non-controlled investment entities recorded at cost. Capital lease obligation of $563,088 for equipment from Royal Bank of Canada entered in 2020. Band has guaranteed member loans totaling $119,860 with $48,285 outstanding. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $2,311,767 + CMHC $57,732 + Social housing $84,375 + Eniyud Health Services $2,261,623 = $4,715,497 (note: Eniyud Health Services may include provincial/federal health funding). OSR estimated as total revenue minus identifiable government transfers.
